reason: Changing the GRUB2 superuser name and updating /etc/grub.d/01_users requires editing bootloader configuration. While not a filesystem partition change, an incorrect edit can prevent the system from booting or lock out admin access, posing a clear risk of loss of console access.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; potential bootloader misconfiguration; possible loss of console/SSH access if superuser password is mis-set; dependency on correct grub2-mkconfig execution
